seoTitle: IXYS News | Product Updates, Technology Announcements | LiTong
seoDescription: Latest news and updates from IXYS including new product releases, technology announcements, and industry insights for power semiconductor solutions.

news.title: IXYS Launches New SiC MOSFET Product Line
news.summary: IXYS introduces Silicon Carbide MOSFETs for high-efficiency power conversion applications in EV charging and renewable energy.
news.content: IXYS Corporation has announced the expansion of its power semiconductor portfolio with the introduction of Silicon Carbide (SiC) MOSFETs. The new product line targets high-efficiency applications including electric vehicle charging infrastructure, solar inverters, and high-frequency power supplies. With voltage ratings from 650V to 1700V and industry-leading on-resistance, these SiC MOSFETs enable system efficiency improvements of 2-3% compared to traditional silicon solutions. The devices feature robust avalanche capability and are available in industry-standard packages for easy integration.

news.title: IXYS Releases 1700V IGBT Series for Industrial Applications
news.summary: New high-voltage IGBT series delivers improved efficiency and ruggedness for motor drives and power conversion.
news.content: IXYS has expanded its IGBT portfolio with a new 1700V series designed for high-voltage industrial applications. The new devices feature advanced trench-gate field-stop technology, delivering low Vce(sat) of 2.0V typical and excellent switching characteristics. Target applications include 690V AC motor drives, high-power solar inverters, and traction systems. The series offers current ratings from 50A to 200A, providing solutions for power levels up to 500kW. Comprehensive short-circuit withstand capability and integrated fast recovery diodes simplify system design.

news.title: IXYS and Littelfuse Showcase Integrated Power Solutions at PCIM
news.summary: Combined portfolio of power semiconductors and circuit protection demonstrated at leading power electronics trade show.
news.content: At the recent PCIM Europe trade show, IXYS and Littelfuse presented their combined portfolio of power semiconductor and circuit protection solutions. The integration of IXYS power devices with Littelfuse protection components offers customers complete system solutions with optimized performance and reliability. Demonstrations included motor drive reference designs, solar inverter solutions, and EV charging systems showcasing the synergies between the two product lines. The combined technical expertise and global distribution network provide enhanced support for customers worldwide.

news.title: IXYS Power Semiconductors Certified for Medical Applications
news.summary: Key power MOSFET and IGBT products receive medical-grade certification for use in life-critical equipment.
news.content: IXYS Corporation announced that several key power semiconductor products have received medical-grade certification in accordance with IEC 60601-1 standards. The certified devices include high-voltage MOSFETs and IGBTs used in medical imaging equipment, surgical tools, and patient monitoring systems. These products undergo enhanced screening and qualification to ensure reliability in life-critical applications. The certification enables medical equipment manufacturers to use IXYS devices with confidence, knowing they meet stringent safety and reliability requirements for healthcare applications.

news.title: IXYS Expands Automotive-Qualified Power Semiconductor Portfolio
news.summary: New AEC-Q101 qualified devices target electric vehicle powertrains and onboard charging systems.
news.content: IXYS has significantly expanded its portfolio of automotive-qualified power semiconductors with new devices targeting electric vehicle applications. The expanded lineup includes AEC-Q101 qualified MOSFETs and IGBTs designed for EV powertrains, onboard chargers, and DC-DC converters. These devices feature enhanced temperature cycling capability, improved moisture resistance, and comprehensive qualification testing. With voltage ratings up to 1200V and current ratings up to 300A, the portfolio addresses the demanding requirements of next-generation electric vehicles.
